name: Mandelbrot/LF
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Coat a cookie sheet with nonstick cooking spray.
Beat eggs, sugar, oil, and vanilla with an electric mixer until smooth.
Add the flour and baking powder; beat until well mixed.
Stir in the chocolate chips (if using). Drop dough by large spoonfuls
along the baking sheet to form 2 vertical lines. The spoonfuls should be
close together.
Using lightly floured hands, work the two lines into parallel rolls lining
each side of the cookie sheet. Leave ample room for spreading between
the rolls and on the sides of the cookie sheets. Each roll will spread
during baking so that it is about 5 inches wide.
Bake rolls for about 20 minutes, or until lightly browned and slightly firm to
the touch. Reduce heat to 300°F.
Cool baked rolls on the sheet for 10 minutes. Using a serrated knife,
cut each roll into 24 slices. Turn each slice on its side as it is cut.
Bake cookies for 10 minutes. Turn slices onto their other sides and return
to the oven for about 5 minutes longer. The cookies on the ends will be done
sooner than the rest. Cool cookies on wire racks.
